facts_disclosed: Saving money aggressively for a truck

turns.content: quick question whats a good app for trackin how much money you save every month

There are a few solid options depending on what you're looking for. Mint is free and tracks spending automatically, YNAB (You Need A Budget) is great for goal-based saving but costs about $15/month, and if you want something simpler, the Ally bank app has a built-in savings bucket feature. What's the main thing you're trying to track?
im tryna save up for a truck bro so I wanna see how fast im gettin there basically. ive been puttin away like half my paycheck every week just grindin it out

Half your paycheck is aggressive, that's solid discipline. For a specific savings goal like a truck, I'd recommend YNAB or even just a simple spreadsheet with a target amount and a tracker. What kind of truck are you looking at and roughly what price range? That'll help figure out how to set up the goal tracking.
im lookin at tacomas or maybe a ranger somethin midsize that can haul tools and stuff. used probably like 25 to 30k range tryna put at least half down so I dont get destroyed on payments

Smart move putting half down -- that'll keep your monthly payment really manageable and you'll get a better interest rate too. So you're looking at saving around $12-15K for the down payment. At half your paycheck each week, depending on what you're making, you could hit that in several months. A basic Google Sheet with your weekly deposits and a running total might honestly be all you need. Want me to outline what that could look like?
yeah thatd be sick honestly I dont need anything fancy I just wanna see the number go up every week and know how far away I am

Here's a simple setup: make a Google Sheet with four columns -- Date, Amount Deposited, Running Total, and Amount Remaining. Put your target (say $15K) at the top. Every week when you deposit, add a row with the date and amount. The Running Total column just adds the new deposit to the previous total, and Amount Remaining subtracts from your goal. You could even add a percentage bar if you want the visual motivation. Takes like 5 minutes to set up.
ok bet im gonna do that tonight. the hardest part honestly is not spendin on dumb stuff like the boys wanna go out every weekend and im sittin here like nah I gotta save

That's the hardest part of any aggressive savings plan for real. One thing that helps is giving yourself a small weekly "fun budget" -- like $50 or whatever you're comfortable with -- so you don't feel completely locked down. If you're too restrictive you'll eventually snap and blow a bunch at once. It's a marathon not a sprint.
thats facts bro last month I didnt go out at all for like 2 weeks and then I went to the bar on a friday and spent way more than I shouldve because I was goin crazy

Yeah that's the classic binge-restrict cycle with money, same thing that happens with diets honestly. Set that weekly fun budget, stick to it guilt-free, and keep stacking the rest toward the truck. You'll get there faster than you think because you won't have those blowout weekends wiping out your progress.

no cap thats actually really good advice im gonna set like 60 bucks a week for goin out and everything else goes straight to savings. im tired of borrowin my dads work truck every time I need to haul somethin

$60 a week for fun is totally reasonable and still leaves you saving hard. And having your own truck when you need to haul stuff is going to be a game changer -- no more asking to borrow one. Keep that motivation in mind every time you're tempted to overspend. You're making a smart play here.
alright bro im gonna set up that spreadsheet rn appreciate the help for real
You got it, good luck with the spreadsheet and the savings grind. At the rate you're going, you'll be truck shopping before you know it. Stay disciplined and you'll get there.
